analysis_description: Round-3 tapes (days 0–2): Black–Scholes implied volatility from option mid and VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; delta and gamma per strike. TTE_days = 8 − tape_day_index per round3work/round3description.txt worked-example pattern (historical day +1→8d, +2→7d, +3→6d aligned to final Round-3 TTE=5d). T in years = TTE_days/365. Subsampled timestamps (step 500). Documented mean IV by strike/day and bid–ask spread (level 1) in summary JSON.
used_to_decide: Near-ATM strikes (5200/5300) show moderate mean IV (~0.23–0.24) and tight spreads vs wings; justified focusing execution on nearest strikes and extract hedge rather than wing IV noise. Equal-weight long strip mean gamma ~0.0092/day supports gamma-scalping thesis magnitude.

analysis_description: Backtester behavior: each historical day reloads Trader with empty traderData, so inferring tape_day from timestamp resets left TTE stuck at 8d for all days. Patched imc-prosperity-4-backtester/prosperity4bt/test_runner.py to inject __BT_TAPE_DAY__ (and __BT_ROUND__) into state.observations.plainValueObservations each tick; traders read this for TTE and day-specific rules. Re-ran all trader versions.
used_to_decide: Without correct tape_day, day-2 PnL and hedge tuning were misleading; after fix, tape day 2 extract hedging showed large negative contribution and v4 (no hedge on day 2) became best among gamma-focused variants.

analysis_description: Additional Greek thread on Round-3 tapes: computed BS-IV from mids for strikes 5100/5200/5300/5400 and summarized near-ATM vs farther strike bins by day (using TTE mapping from round3description pattern). Near-ATM options showed consistently higher average gamma (~0.00212, 0.00221, 0.00237 from day0->day2) than farther strikes (~0.00157, 0.00159, 0.00160), while average option delta stayed around 0.50. Also checked extract realized absolute move per tick by day (~0.85 on all days).
used_to_decide: This supported keeping focus on the two nearest strikes (gamma density) and testing inventory-hold / gamma-threshold sweeps rather than adding far-wing vouchers or hydrogel-heavy logic.

analysis_description: BS vega and theta (per calendar day) from implied vol solved on mids, for the two nearest strikes to extract at each subsampled tick (step 500). TTE 8/7/6 for tape days 0/1/2 per round3work/round3description.txt pattern. Outputs: vega_theta_summary.json (mean vega and mean |theta|/day by tape day) and vega_theta_sample.csv. Script: analyze_vega_theta.py.
used_to_decide: Mean BS vega on nearest two strikes falls from ~298 to ~254 as TTE shortens (days 0->2) while per-day |theta| rises ~4.3 to ~5.0, consistent with time decay building into shorter horizon. Suggested day-2 quote tightening (v8) but backtest with match-trades worse got zero VEV fills when MM_EDGE=0 (quotes at best bid/ask, no price improvement for taker). Day-2 requote speed sweep (v9) matched v4 PnL; keep v4 as simplest best.

analysis_description: IV vs extract co-movement: on each tape day, subsample every 100th timestamp in time order; compute BS implied vol from nearest-strike VEV mid and extract mid (TTE 8/7/6). Pearson correlation between successive differences d(IV) and d(S) on the subseries (n≈99 pairs/day). Script: analyze_iv_extract_comovement.py. Output: analysis_outputs/iv_deltaS_corr.json.
used_to_decide: Correlation is weak/mixed (day0 ~-0.04, days1-2 ~+0.28–0.31), so IV level from EMA is still a reasonable fair; no strong case to add an extract→IV overlay signal. Global requote speed sweep (v10) did not beat v4.
